Author: .leonmc
Content:
There's a company creating ASICs with LLM models hardcoded into the chip, so it can run only that specific model, but much faster and more efficiently: https://taalas.com/

Out of curiosity, is this something that could be done (on a smaller scale) on wafer.space? I feel like the low cost might be a big advantage here, and the old process node not as much of a disadvantage as for other types of chips (since the main comparison is GPUs, not other model specific ASICs, so even an old node might be good enough).

I have no idea how to estimate how large the chip would need to be depending on parameter count, so maybe this is completely unrealistic and a stupid question 😅
